name: Bedrock
notes: Unbreakable and unobtainable in survival. Blocks piston extension entirely (piston does not extend). Explicitly excluded from normal mob spawning despite being a solid opaque block.

name: Budding Amethyst
notes: Cannot be mined/silk-touched (yields nothing when broken by hand). Breaks immediately if pushed OR pulled by any piston, despite needing no support.

name: Amethyst Cluster (+ Small/Medium/Large Buds)
height_note: Grows with stage: ~3px (small bud) up to ~7px (full cluster)
width_note: Footprint grows with stage: small/medium buds are narrower, widening to ~10px×10px for a full cluster, centered on the attachment face.
notes: Must grow from/attach to Budding Amethyst or another cluster segment.

name: Moss Block
notes: Full opaque block that spreads via bone meal. In Java Edition it is destroyed and dropped when pushed by a piston (movable in Bedrock Edition).

name: Sculk
notes: Spreads via a Sculk Catalyst consuming XP nearby. An ordinary movable full block, unlike Sculk Sensor/Shrieker/Catalyst.

name: Sculk Vein
notes: Thin coating that spreads across any adjacent block face(s), like Glow Lichen. No player collision.

name: Obsidian
notes: Blocks piston extension entirely. High blast resistance.

name: Crying Obsidian
notes: Blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Reinforced Deepslate
notes: Unbreakable and unobtainable in survival. Blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Resin Clump
notes: Thin decorative growth on certain blocks (Pale Garden). No player collision.

name: Mud
height_note: Top surface sits 2px below a full block (collision box 16×14×16) even though the visual model looks flush.

name: Grass Block
notes: The primary block passive mobs (animals) naturally spawn on in the Overworld.

name: Farmland
notes: Top sits 1px below a full block. Reverts to Dirt if the space above is obstructed or it's trampled by a jumping entity. Partial block (15/16 height) — explicitly not a valid spawn surface per the wiki.

name: Dirt Path
notes: Top sits 1px below a full block. Reverts to Dirt if a block is placed on top. Partial block (15/16 height), same mechanic as Farmland — not a valid spawn surface.

name: Ores — Coal, Iron, Copper, Gold, Redstone, Emerald, Lapis, Diamond (+ Deepslate variants), Nether Gold, Nether Quartz
notes: Lit Redstone Ore behaves identically to unlit for these properties.

name: Ancient Debris
notes: Very high blast resistance, but a normal movable/stable block for these properties.

name: Mineral Storage Blocks — Coal, Iron, Copper, Gold, Redstone, Emerald, Lapis, Diamond, Netherite, Raw Iron, Raw Copper, Raw Gold
notes: Block of Redstone is the one exception in this group — it is explicitly non-conductive (so it can't re-power itself into an infinite loop).

name: Copper Block (+ Cut, all oxidation stages, waxed)
Copper Bulb (all oxidation stages)
notes: Toggles its lit state via redstone; physical properties unaffected.

name: Copper Grate (all oxidation stages)
notes: Solid but not opaque (light passes through the lattice), so it isn't a valid mob-spawn surface.

name: Nether Bricks (+ Cracked, Chiseled, Red Nether Bricks)
Soul Sand
height_note: Top surface sits 2px below a full block (collision box 16×14×16) even though the visual model looks flush.
notes: Slows entities standing on it; creates upward bubble columns underwater that pull entities/items down.

name: Soul Soil
notes: Extends Soul Fire when set alight nearby; does not slow entities (unlike Soul Sand).

name: Blackstone (+ Polished, Bricks, Cracked, Chiseled, Gilded)
Magma Block
notes: Damages standing entities (unless wearing Frost Walker-affected boots or immune) but is still a normal opaque spawn surface.

name: Glowstone
notes: Transparent — monsters (incl. slimes) cannot spawn on it, same as Sea Lantern.

name: Nether Wart Block (+ Warped Wart Block)
notes: Warped Wart Block is the exception — not flammable.

name: Respawn Anchor
notes: Blocks piston extension entirely. Explodes if activated without charges in the Overworld/End.

name: End Gateway
notes: No collision — entities fall through into the teleport field. Only placed by world generation/commands.

name: End Portal (block)
notes: No collision. Only placed by world generation/commands.

name: End Portal Frame
notes: Slightly short of a full block (13/16px). Blocks piston extension entirely. Raised rim / hollow eye-socket shape — not a complete flat top.

name: Sea Lantern
notes: Transparent — monsters (incl. slimes) cannot spawn on it, same as Glowstone.

name: Conduit
notes: Not a full cube — it's a small 6×6×6px floating cube centered in the block (doesn't touch the floor), not a 16px cube. Not opaque, so it's not a valid spawn surface. Blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Coral Block (all 5 colors, live + dead)
notes: Live coral blocks die (become their dead variant) if not adjacent to water.

name: Coral / Coral Fan (all 5 colors, live + dead, incl. wall fans)
notes: Special piston exception like Rails — usually relocates instead of breaking, though some configurations still break it and emit a block update. Needs a solid block below/beside; live coral also needs adjacent water to stay alive.

name: Kelp (+ Kelp Plant)
notes: Grows upward as a multi-block stack; base needs a solid block or more Kelp below.

name: Seagrass (+ Tall Seagrass)
notes: Needs to be underwater on top of a solid block.

name: Sea Pickle (1–4 cluster)
notes: Has a real (if small) collision box — roughly 4×6×4px for a single pickle, growing somewhat with cluster size. Needs a solid block below; glows brighter underwater.

name: Frogspawn
notes: Must sit on top of Water with a solid block below the water. Hatches into tadpoles over time.

name: Ice
notes: Transparent, so it isn't a valid opaque spawn surface despite being solid. Melts near light unless in a cold-enough biome.

name: Frosted Ice
notes: Gradually melts back to Water unless kept cold; otherwise behaves like normal Ice. Transparent like Ice — not a valid spawn surface (only Polar Bears are exempt).

name: Snow Layer (1–7 layers)
height_note: Collision height is 2px LESS than the visual layer height (16×(2L-2)×16): a single layer (L=1) has almost no collision at all, up to 12px collision at 7 layers; an 8th layer becomes a full Snow Block instead.
notes: Needs a solid block below. Polar bears are a special-cased exception that can still spawn on snow/ice.

name: Logs & Wood (all species incl. Crimson/Warped stems, stripped variants, all axis orientations)
notes: Crimson and Warped are the exception — Nether "wood" (their Stems, Hyphae, and stripped variants) is not flammable and cannot be set alight, unlike all Overworld log/wood species.

name: Planks (all species incl. Bamboo Planks & Bamboo Mosaic)
notes: Crimson and Warped Planks are the exception — Nether wood is not flammable, unlike all Overworld plank species (Bamboo Planks & Mosaic included).

name: Chiseled Bookshelf
notes: Stores books (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely, like a chest.

name: Crafting Table / Cartography Table / Fletching Table / Smithing Table / Loom / Stonecutter
notes: Plain functional blocks with no stored inventory — movable normally. Smithing Table and Stonecutter are the exceptions — not flammable.

name: Leaves (all species incl. Azalea/Flowering Azalea Leaves)
notes: Persists based on distance-to-log (not physical support), so it's stable/floating-capable. Destroyed (not relocated) when pushed by a piston, and does NOT stick to slime/honey blocks. Can be waterlogged, so flowing water doesn't wash it away.

name: Saplings (all species, incl. Mangrove Propagule)
notes: Needs Dirt/Podzol/Mud/Farmland-type block below; needs light to grow.

name: Carpet (16 colors + Moss Carpet)
notes: Needs a non-air block below (any block qualifies), but this is a named piston exception — carpets relocate intact instead of breaking. Still washed away by flowing water. Moss Carpet is the exception — not flammable.

name: Concrete Powder (16 colors)
notes: Turns into solid Concrete instantly on contact with water.

name: Glazed Terracotta (16 colors)
notes: Movable by piston, but uniquely does NOT stick to adjacent slime/honey blocks the way most movable blocks do. Conductive like any full block, on top of its other quirks noted above.

name: Glass (clear + 16 stained colors)
notes: Transparent — not a valid opaque spawn surface.

name: Glass Pane (clear + 16 stained) / Iron Bars / Copper Bars
width_note: Thin 2px-wide post by default; visually (and physically) widens up to 14–16px where it connects to neighboring panes/bars/blocks.
notes: Thin cross-shaped hitbox (connects visually to neighbors) but still blocks movement through the space. Needs no support. Copper Bars (added 1.21.9) oxidize but share these properties.

name: Stairs, right-side-up (all ~40 materials)
notes: Full-height stepped collision box, but the stepped top is not a flat opaque 1×1 face, so mobs can't spawn on it. Needs no supporting block.

name: Stairs, upside-down (all ~40 materials)
notes: Flipped orientation — the flat face is on top, which DOES count as an opaque spawn surface (Java Edition). Needs no supporting block.

name: Slab, bottom half (all ~40 materials)
notes: Sits in the lower half of the block space. Needs no supporting block.

name: Slab, top half (all ~40 materials)
notes: Sits in the upper half — its flat top counts as an opaque spawn surface even though the block is only 8px thick. Needs no supporting block.

name: Slab, double (two slabs combined = full block)
notes: Functionally identical to a full block once combined.

name: Walls (all ~15 materials)
height_note: Collision height is uniformly 24px (1.5 blocks), whether the wall segment is an isolated post or connected to neighbors — taller than the visual model specifically to stop mobs hopping over.
width_note: Center post footprint is ~8px×8px; connecting arms extend outward toward neighbors, similar to fences.
notes: Not a full 1×1 cube, so it isn't a valid opaque spawn surface. The 24px collision height (taller than the block itself) is what blocks most mobs from hopping over, even across connected runs.

name: Fences (all wood types + Nether Brick Fence)
height_note: 1.5 blocks tall for collision (prevents jumping over), though the visual post is narrower than a full block
width_note: 4px×4px post by default; connecting sides extend the footprint toward neighbors (up to 6px in the connecting direction).
notes: Connects visually to adjacent fences, fence gates, and solid blocks.

name: Fence Gates (all wood types)
width_note: Closed collision box is 16px long × 4px thick × 24px tall; the 4px thickness is shown here to match the convention used for doors and other thin blocks. Opening the gate removes its collision entirely.
notes: Solid 1.5-block-tall barrier when closed; zero collision at all when open.

name: Doors (all wood types + Iron Door + Copper Door)
width_note: Only ~3px thick as a swung panel, though it spans the full 16px width/height of the doorway it's mounted in.
notes: Two-block-tall object (bottom & top are separate block states). The bottom half needs a solid block below it — pistons break it rather than relocating it. Copper Doors (openable by hand and by redstone) oxidize but share these properties.

name: Trapdoors (all wood types + Iron Trapdoor + Copper Trapdoor)
height_note: ~3px when closed against a floor/ceiling; effectively 16px (vertical) when open
notes: Attaches to the block face it's mounted against. Copper Trapdoors oxidize but share these properties.

name: Signs (standing + wall, all wood types) / Hanging Signs
notes: Standing/wall signs attach to a floor or wall block; Hanging Signs attach to a ceiling block, fence, or wall below. No player collision.

name: Banners (standing + wall, 16 colors)
notes: Standing banners attach to the floor; wall banners attach to a wall.

name: Redstone Dust (Wire)
notes: Needs a solid block below; connects to adjacent power sources/dust/blocks.

name: Redstone Torch
notes: Attaches to the top or side of an opaque block.

name: Redstone Repeater
notes: Needs a solid block below; thin but standable hitbox (unlike dust/torches).

name: Redstone Comparator
notes: Needs a solid block below; thin but standable hitbox.

name: Lever
notes: Attaches to the block face it was placed against (floor, wall, or ceiling).

name: Buttons (all wood types, Stone, Polished Blackstone)
notes: Attaches to the block face it was placed against.

name: Pressure Plates (all wood types, Stone, Polished Blackstone, Heavy Weighted/Iron, Light Weighted/Gold)
notes: Has NO collision box at all in Java Edition (officially listed as noCollision) — you're actually resting on the solid block underneath; the plate just depresses visually and triggers on proximity/weight. Needs a solid block below.

name: Tripwire Hook
notes: Attaches to the side of a solid block, aimed inward toward the tripwire line.

name: Tripwire (string)
notes: Needs a solid block below; connects between two Tripwire Hooks.

name: Note Block
notes: Purely blockstate-based (no stored NBT) — freely movable, unlike Jukebox.

name: Piston / Sticky Piston (retracted)
notes: Movable like a normal block while retracted. The extended piston, its Piston Head, and the transient 'Moving Piston' block are separate immovable technical blocks that only exist mid-animation.

name: Dropper / Dispenser
notes: Stores items (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely, like a chest.

name: Hopper
notes: Stores items — blocks piston extension entirely. Funnel-shaped top with a hole in the middle — explicitly named as not a valid spawn surface.

name: Lodestone
notes: Blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Calibrated Sculk Sensor / Sculk Sensor / Sculk Shrieker / Sculk Catalyst
notes: Blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Daylight Detector
notes: Shorter than a full block (6/16px); doesn't need a supporting block. Domed sensor top, not a flat full face.

name: Crafter
notes: Stores items (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely.

name: TNT
notes: Sits like a normal block; becomes a separate falling/flying 'Primed TNT' entity only once ignited. Ignites instantly and detonates rather than smoldering like a flammable block.

name: Slime Block
notes: Sticky — dragging it with a piston also drags adjacent movable blocks with it. Bounces entities. Translucent, but explicitly named as an allowed mob-spawn surface despite not being opaque.

name: Honey Block
height_note: Collision box is 14×14×15px (width×length×height) — not a full 16px cube. The 15px height (1px shy of full) is why a block placed on top sits slightly low.
width_note: Horizontal footprint is 14×14px (1px inset on each side), which is why arrows and thrown items can slip through the 2px gap between adjacent honey blocks.
notes: Sticky like Slime Block (the two don't stick to each other). Slows movement and negates fall damage on contact. Translucent — not a valid spawn surface.

name: Rails (Regular, Powered, Detector, Activator)
notes: Special piston exception, like Carpet — normally relocates intact and re-orients itself instead of breaking, as long as it keeps a full-faced support block. Destroyed by flowing water OR flowing lava. Rare edge cases with perpendicular pistons can still break it.

name: Grass & Fern (short)
notes: Needs Grass Block, Dirt, Coarse Dirt, Podzol, or Mycelium below.

name: Tall Grass & Large Fern (2-block tall)
notes: Each half is a separate block state; needs the grass-type block below.

name: Small Flowers (Dandelion, Poppy, Tulips, Orchid, Allium, Azure Bluet, Oxeye Daisy, Cornflower, Lily of the Valley, Wither Rose, Torchflower, Eyeblossom)
notes: Needs a dirt/grass/farmland-type block below (Wither Rose also grows on Nether soil/Soul Sand; it damages nearby entities).

name: Tall Flowers (Sunflower, Lilac, Rose Bush, Peony, Pitcher Plant)
notes: Two-block-tall; each half is a separate block state.

name: Crops (Wheat, Carrots, Potatoes, Beetroot, Torchflower Crop, Pitcher Crop)
notes: Needs tilled Farmland below.

name: Nether Wart
notes: Needs Soul Sand or Soul Soil below — no tilling required.

name: Pumpkin & Melon Stems (+ Attached variants)
notes: Needs Farmland below; grows a Pumpkin/Melon on an adjacent block.

name: Sweet Berry Bush
notes: Damages entities walking through it (except foxes/bees); needs a dirt/grass-type block below.

name: Sugar Cane
notes: Needs Sand/Dirt/Grass-type block directly adjacent to water, or another Sugar Cane below.

name: Bamboo (+ Bamboo Sapling, Bamboo Shoot)
width_note: Slim 3×16×3px stalk — much narrower than its leafy interaction box (6px, or 10px for large leaves).
notes: Unlike most plants, Bamboo IS solid with real collision. Needs Dirt/Sand/Mud/Gravel-type block or Bamboo below.

name: Cactus
width_note: 14×15×14px collision box, inset 1px from a full block on all four sides (same 14px footprint as a Honey Block).
notes: Needs Sand/Red Sand/another Cactus below with no solid block on any of its 4 sides. Damages entities that touch it.

name: Lily Pad
height_note: Precisely 1.5px tall; rounded up to the nearest whole pixel here.
notes: Must rest on Water or Ice. 14×1.5×14px collision box, inset ~1px on the four side faces. Players and most mobs can walk on top without sinking, despite the thin hitbox.

name: Small Mushrooms (Red, Brown)
notes: Needs an opaque block below; grows only at light level ≤ 12 (or any light on Mycelium/Podzol/Nylium).

name: Mushroom Blocks (Red/Brown Huge Mushroom, all cap/stem/pore variants + Mushroom Stem)
Crimson Fungus / Warped Fungus
notes: Nether equivalent of a mushroom; needs Nylium, Netherrack, Soul Soil, or Mycelium below.

name: Crimson Roots / Warped Roots
notes: Purely decorative Nether equivalent of grass.

name: Weeping Vines (+ Plant) / Twisting Vines (+ Plant)
notes: Weeping Vines hang from Nylium/Netherrack ceilings; Twisting Vines grow upward from the floor.

name: Vines
notes: Attaches to and spreads across solid block faces; no player collision.

name: Cave Vines (+ Plant, with/without Glow Berries)
notes: Hangs downward from a ceiling block; each segment needs the block/vine above it.

name: Glow Lichen
notes: Attaches to any adjacent block face(s) it was grown/placed on.

name: Hanging Roots
notes: Hangs from a solid block above (found under Lush Caves).

name: Big Dripleaf (+ Stem)
height_note: The leaf pad's real collision is only 4px tall when stable, 2px mid-tilt, and empty (no collision at all) at full tilt — the supporting stem itself has zero collision.
notes: Tilts and briefly drops entities standing on it. Needs a solid block, its own Stem, or another Big Dripleaf below.

name: Small Dripleaf
notes: Needs Moss Block or a similar dirt-type block below; 2 blocks tall.

name: Azalea (+ Flowering Azalea)
notes: Needs a dirt-type, Moss, or Clay block below. The bushy canopy in the upper half reaches all 4 edges and the full top of the block, giving it a full 16px-tall collision box overall, even though the lower half is just a thin 4px×4px stem.

name: Chorus Plant (+ Chorus Flower)
notes: Grows only on/from End Stone; each segment needs a connected Chorus Plant/End Stone below it.

name: Pointed Dripstone
width_note: Footprint varies by 'thickness' state (tip/frustum/middle/base/merge): as narrow as ~6px at a tip/merge, widening toward ~12px+ at a base segment. 6px shown here for the common tip/merge stages.
notes: Grows from a Dripstone Block on the floor (stalagmite) or ceiling (stalactite). Deals fall damage; also a gravity block once unsupported.

name: Water (source + flowing)
notes: A piston can't push liquid — it only obstructs/displaces it. Not itself a mob-spawn surface (aquatic mobs spawn IN it, not on it).

name: Lava (source + flowing)
notes: Damages/ignites entities on contact. Flowing lava destroys many of the same items flowing water does (rails, redstone dust, etc.), plus sets flammable blocks alight.

name: Powder Snow
notes: Entities sink into and can suffocate in it unless wearing leather boots (or are immune, e.g. rabbits/foxes); freezes exposed entities over time. Obtained/placed via bucket.

name: Pumpkin / Carved Pumpkin / Jack o'Lantern
notes: In Java Edition, Pumpkins / Carved Pumpkins / Jack o'Lanterns break and drop when pushed by a piston (the mechanic piston pumpkin/melon farms rely on); movable in Bedrock.

name: Melon
notes: In Java Edition, a Melon breaks and drops when pushed by a piston; movable in Bedrock.

name: Composter
notes: Slightly shorter-looking due to the open basin, but the collision box is a full 16px cube (like a cauldron/hopper) — you cannot fall into it by walking over the top. No stored NBT that blocks pistons — movable normally. Not opaque, so it's not a valid spawn surface.

name: Cauldron (+ Water/Lava/Powder Snow variants)
notes: Looks like a hollow basin with ~6px walls, but the actual collision box is a full 16px cube — you walk straight over the top instead of falling in, the same trick used by the Composter and Hopper. Interaction (filling/emptying) still targets the inner basin via a separate auxiliary hitbox.

name: Chain (Iron + Copper, all oxidation stages)
width_note: Thin ~3px×3px vertical link, despite occupying the full 16px height of the block.
notes: Thin vertical connector; unlike a Lantern it can float freely without support. Renamed from 'chain' to 'iron_chain' in 1.21.9, which also added the oxidizing Copper Chain (same properties). Thin ~3px link, nowhere near a full top face.

name: Cobweb
notes: Has NO collision box at all in Java Edition (officially listed as noCollision) — entities pass straight through and are merely slowed by a velocity penalty, they don't stand on or in it. Unlike most attachment blocks, Cobweb needs no support and breaks (rather than relocates) when pushed by a piston. Mobs can still spawn INSIDE a cobweb if the block below qualifies.

name: Dragon Egg
notes: Teleports away when clicked. Breaks (pops as an item) when pushed by a piston, despite needing no support — and is also gravity-affected. 14×16×14px collision box, inset ~1px on the four side faces only.

name: Turtle Egg (1–4 cluster)
width_note: ~9px footprint for the egg cluster itself (not inset like a full block); multi-egg clusters share this same footprint, just with more eggs modeled inside it.
notes: Needs Sand or Red Sand below.

name: Scaffolding
notes: Gravity-affected, but only falls once more than 6 blocks (through connected scaffolding) from the nearest anchor. Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston unless it is in a falling state. Players can descend by holding crouch.

name: Ladder
width_note: Very thin (~3px) panel flush against the wall it's mounted on.
notes: Attaches to the side of a solid block. Solid but thin; lets entities climb. Can be waterlogged, so it survives water.

name: End Rod
width_note: Thin ~4px×4px rod along its axis (unlike a Lantern's cage-like footprint, the rod itself has no wider cap in the collision box).
notes: Attaches to the block face it's placed against, along its own axis.

name: Bed (all 16 colors)
notes: Two-block object (head + foot). Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston. Explodes if used in the Nether or End. Low-profile mattress + leg shape, not a flat full-height top.

name: Mob Heads (Skeleton, Wither Skeleton, Zombie, Creeper, Piglin, Dragon, Player)
width_note: Head-shaped footprint, roughly 8×8px, regardless of mounting orientation.
notes: Explicitly washed away by flowing water. Needs a solid block below (floor-standing) or behind (wall-mounted).

name: Flower Pot (+ potted plants)
notes: Has a real (if small) 6×6×6px collision box — you bump into it rather than passing through. Needs a solid block below; explicitly washed away by flowing water.

name: Cake
width_note: 14×8×14px collision box — inset 1px from a full block on each side.
notes: Needs a solid block below.

name: Candle (1–4 stacked, all colors + uncolored)
width_note: Thin ~2px×2px wick cluster at the center of the block, regardless of how many candles (1–4) are stacked.
notes: Needs a solid block below.

name: Decorated Pot
width_note: 14×16×14px collision box — full block height, inset ~1px on the four side faces only.
notes: Holds a single item (block entity). Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston, and shatters into sherds when hit by a projectile. Full-height collision box despite the open neck at the top.

name: Anvil (+ Chipped, Damaged)
width_note: Non-cubic, rotation-dependent footprint: ~12px along the short axis, a full 16px along the long axis (the 'feet' stick out to the block edges in one direction).
notes: A placed Anvil blocks piston extension entirely (Java Edition). A FALLING anvil mid-drop is a separate entity that CAN be pushed by a piston (but not pulled). Non-cubic footprint. Non-cubic anvil shape (narrow base/wide top varies by axis), not a full flat top.

name: Grindstone
width_note: Angled frame-and-wheel shape; overall footprint is ~12px×16px depending on axis — 12px is the narrower representative dimension.
notes: Angled, non-cubic shape, but solid. Non-cubic frame-and-wheel shape (partial widths on every face) — not a full flat top.

name: Enchanting Table
notes: Shorter than a full block and not opaque on top.

name: Beacon
notes: Transparent (light and its beam pass through) despite being solid.

name: Furnace / Blast Furnace / Smoker
notes: Stores items/fuel — blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Chest / Trapped Chest
notes: Collision box is a 14×14×14px cube, inset ~1px from a full block on every side (not a full 16×16×16). Stores items — blocks piston extension entirely. A double chest separates into two singles if forced apart. Not a valid spawn surface.

name: Ender Chest
notes: Collision box is a 14×14×14px cube, inset ~1px from a full block on every side (not a full 16×16×16) — same shape as a regular Chest. Blocks piston extension entirely. Not a valid spawn surface.

name: Barrel
notes: Stores items — blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Lectern
height_note: Base plate collision is only ~2px tall; the slanted reading-stand column brings the effective collision up to ~14px on the tall side. Not a flat full-height top.
notes: Holds a book (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely. Sloped reading-stand top, not a flat face.

name: Beehive / Bee Nest
notes: Holds bees/honey (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely. Flammable (added in snapshot 19w35a/19w42a) despite storing bees/honey.

name: Jukebox
notes: Holds an inserted disc — blocks piston extension entirely.

name: Bell
width_note: 16px along the axis the bell frame faces, but only ~8px thick perpendicular to that — 8px is the representative 'thin' dimension, matching how Fences/Walls are recorded.
notes: Mounted on a floor, wall (single/double), or ceiling. Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston. Bell hangs within an open frame, not a flat full top.

name: Lantern (+ Soul Lantern, Copper Lantern)
height_note: Same 9px total height whether hanging from a block above or sitting on a block below — only the vertical position within the block shifts.
width_note: Cage-shaped footprint, roughly 6×7×6px, whether hanging from above or sitting below.
notes: Hangs from a block above, or sits on a block below. Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston. Open cage shape, not a flat full top. Copper Lantern (added 1.21.9) oxidizes but shares these properties.

name: Campfire (+ Soul Campfire)
notes: Holds up to 4 cooking items (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely. Water extinguishes it (turns unlit) without destroying the block. Log/fire pile shape, not a flat full top.

name: Brewing Stand
notes: Holds potion ingredients/fuel (tile entity) — blocks piston extension entirely. Collision box is a 14px-tall, 14px-wide union of the thin base plate (2px) and the central rod (up to 14px) — taller overall than it first appears, though most of that footprint is a thin 2px×2px column.

name: Barrier
notes: Invisible in survival. Solid and blocks piston extension.

name: Structure Void
notes: No collision; used as an 'eraser' inside structure templates. Breaks when pushed by a piston in Java Edition.

name: Light Block
notes: Invisible light-level block, obtainable via commands/creative inventory.

name: Trial Spawner
notes: Like Monster Spawner, spawns its own mobs via special line-of-sight logic around itself, not via the normal top-surface rule.

name: Monster Spawner
notes: Spawns mobs INSIDE its own space via unique spawner logic, not on top of it via normal surface rules.

name: Test Block / Test Instance Block
notes: Creator/testing tool blocks, obtainable only via commands.

name: Nether Portal
notes: No collision; teleports entities that touch it.

name: Piston Head / Moving Piston (technical states)
notes: Transient blocks that exist only mid-animation while a piston is extended; not independently obtainable.

name: Sandstone / Red Sandstone (+ Chiseled, Cut, Smooth)
notes: Full opaque building blocks; both colors and all Chiseled/Cut/Smooth variants share these properties.

name: Bricks
notes: The plain clay Bricks block (crafted from four Brick items).

name: Infested Blocks (Stone, Cobblestone, Stone Bricks +Mossy/Cracked/Chiseled, Deepslate)
notes: Identical to their host block for these properties, but release a Silverfish when broken and are mined much faster. Cannot be obtained with Silk Touch.

name: Pale Moss Block
notes: Full opaque block (Pale Garden). Like Moss Block, it breaks and drops when pushed by a piston in Java Edition (movable in Bedrock).

name: Muddy Mangrove Roots
notes: Full opaque block, unlike the non-opaque Mangrove Roots it is crafted from.

name: Mangrove Roots
notes: A full cube but non-opaque (doesn't suffocate mobs), so it's not a valid spawn surface and doesn't conduct redstone. Can be waterlogged despite being a full block; flammable.

name: Shelf (all wood types)
width_note: Wall-mounted panel — 16px wide and tall but only 5px deep.
notes: Stores up to 3 item stacks (block entity) — blocks piston extension entirely, like a chest. When powered by redstone, interacting swaps the shelf's contents with the player's hotbar; adjacent powered shelves connect. Added in 1.21.9 (The Copper Age). Crimson and Warped Shelves are the exception — Nether wood is not flammable.

name: Tinted Glass
notes: Full cube that blocks ALL light (unlike regular Glass) yet is still non-opaque for spawning/redstone — mobs can't spawn on it and it doesn't conduct.

name: Copper Golem Statue (all oxidation stages, waxed)
width_note: Statue shape ~10×14×10px, not a full cube.
notes: Decorative poseable statue — cycles through 4 poses when used. Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston; doesn't stick to slime/honey. Oxidizes over time and can be waxed. Added in 1.21.9.

name: Dried Kelp Block
notes: Full opaque block; flammable (burns away) and usable as furnace fuel.

name: Hay Block
notes: Full opaque block; reduces fall damage for entities landing on it. Flammable. Rotatable pillar (axis states).

name: Honeycomb Block
notes: Full opaque decorative block; not flammable despite being made of honeycomb.

name: Froglight (Ochre, Verdant, Pearlescent)
notes: Full opaque light-emitting block (light level 15). Unlike Glowstone/Sea Lantern it IS opaque, so it conducts redstone and counts as a spawn surface.

name: Shulker Box (uncolored + 16 colors)
notes: Portable storage that keeps its contents when broken. A full cube but non-opaque (no spawns on top, non-conductive). Breaks and drops as an item when pushed by a piston; cannot be pulled. Opening needs ~half a block of clearance above its top face.

name: Copper Chest (all oxidation stages, waxed)
width_note: 14×14×14px cube, inset ~1px on every side (like a regular Chest).
notes: Stores items (block entity) — blocks piston extension entirely. Two placed side by side form a large copper chest. Oxidizes over time and can be waxed. Added in 1.21.9.

name: Heavy Core
width_note: Small 8×8×8px core centered in the block, like a Conduit.
notes: Very high blast resistance; used to craft the Mace. Movable by pistons but does not stick to slime/honey blocks.

name: Sniffer Egg
width_note: ~12–14px footprint, full 16px tall.
notes: Hatches into a Sniffer over time (faster on Moss Block). Breaks and drops when pushed by a piston.

name: Dried Ghast
width_note: ~10×10×10px block, not a full cube.
notes: Rehydrates in water over four stages and eventually spawns a Happy Ghast. Waterloggable. Added in 1.21.6.

name: Lightning Rod (all oxidation stages, waxed)
width_note: Thin 4×4px rod spanning the full 16px height along its axis.
notes: Emits a redstone signal when struck by lightning and attracts nearby strikes. Attaches to a block face; waterloggable. Oxidizes (as of 1.21.9) and can be waxed.

name: Cocoa (Cocoa Pods)
height_note: Grows with age: ~5px tall (age 0) up to ~9px at full growth.
width_note: Footprint grows from ~4px to ~6px with age.
notes: Grows on the side of Jungle Logs/Wood through three stages. Has a small real collision box.

name: Spore Blossom
notes: Hangs from a solid block above; drips water particles. No collision.

name: Pale Hanging Moss
notes: Hangs beneath Pale Moss Block / other blocks above (Pale Garden). No collision.

name: Nether Sprouts
notes: Small decorative Nether plant; needs Nylium/Soul Soil-type block below. No collision.

name: Ground Cover (Pink Petals, Wildflowers, Leaf Litter)
notes: Flat 1–4 segment ground cover that needs a solid block below. No collision. Pink Petals (Cherry Grove); Wildflowers & Leaf Litter (Spring to Life).

name: Dry Vegetation (Bush, Firefly Bush, Short & Tall Dry Grass)
notes: Decorative dry vegetation (Spring to Life / badlands). Firefly Bush emits firefly particles at night. No collision.

name: Cactus Flower
notes: Small flower that grows on top of / beside Cactus (Spring to Life). No collision.

name: Torch / Soul Torch / Copper Torch (+ wall variants)
notes: Light source that attaches to the top or side of a block; no collision and washed away by water. Copper Torch added in 1.21.9. (The Redstone Torch is listed separately.)
